mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, triggering inflation expectations that force the Fed to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE sustains below 4.0% for two consecutive quarters, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity outperformance over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the partial correlation of RSAFS and INDPRO with SPX returns drops below 0.10 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, real yields are driven by inflation expectations rather than policy rates alone. When CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS, real rates fall, supporting equity valuations despite nominal tightening over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS growth during rate hikes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly expands corporate revenue bases, translating into higher earnings per share and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate profitability tracks macroeconomic expansion.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward SPX returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window, or earnings growth decouples from GDP growth for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral following monthly UNRATE jumps greater than 0.5pp, or the signal's hit rate falls below 40% in walk-forward testing across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline while industrial production (INDPRO) remains elevated, it indicates a rotation from construction to manufacturing activity, boosting corporate capex expectations and driving short-term equity gains over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between HOUST declines and INDPRO stability shows no predictive power for SPX forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive Fed tightening, equity valuations are highly sensitive to labor cost pressures. When PAYEMS growth accelerates alongside rising FEDFUNDS, profit margins compress, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not decline when PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing with Rank IC below 0.02.
